summary: Cheetah 1.0 is a fully-featured single-page proxy browser app with an amethyst/purple high-tech theme. It includes tab-based browsing with iframe proxy (allorigins.win), a Google CSE search bar, an unblocked games page, an AI homework helper (Puter.js), a code editor/presentation/website builder, and a quick-open links page. Built as a single index.html with embedded CSS and JS.
architectural_notes: Single index.html with embedded CSS and JS, no build tools
Iframe proxy uses allorigins.win CORS proxy: https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=
Amethyst/purple glassmorphism theme with neon glow effects and monospace fonts
Puter.js (window.puter.ai.chat) used for AI homework helper
LocalStorage used for code editor content and app settings
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+T (new tab), Ctrl+W (close tab), Ctrl+L (focus address bar)
Panic button: triggered by click or Cmd/Ctrl+P or B; shows white screen overlay and blocks access for 60 seconds using localStorage timestamp

existing_features.title: Create a single-page React application named 'Cheetah 1.0' with a persistent amethyst/purple high-tech theme: deep purples (#6C3483), violets (#9B59B6), and magenta (#8E44AD) gradients; glassmorphism panels with backdrop blur; neon glow effects on interactive elements; animated gradient backgrounds; and a sleek monospace font. Display 'Cheetah 1.0' prominently in the header alongside a cheetah icon/logo. Dark mode is the default and only mode.

Implement a sidebar (or top navigation bar) with five navigation destinations: Home/Search, Games, AI Assistant, Code Editor, and Quick Open. Navigating between them updates the main content area without a page reload. Include smooth transitions between pages.

Implement a persistent tab bar above the main content area that supports multiple open iframe tabs simultaneously. Each tab shows the page title, favicon, and a close (×) button. A '+' new-tab button is always visible. Tabs maintain independent iframe state. Support keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+T (new tab), Ctrl+W (close active tab), Ctrl+L (focus address bar).

Build the Home/Search page. Include: (1) An address bar that accepts full URLs; (2) A Google Custom Search Engine (CSE ID: 464f1cf85006c4770) search bar; (3) Back, Forward, and Refresh navigation buttons for the active iframe; (4) When a URL is entered or a search result is clicked, load the target URL inside an iframe tab using the allorigins.win CORS proxy (`https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=<encoded-url>`) as the iframe src; (5) A loading spinner while the iframe loads; (6) An error message with a direct link fallback when the site cannot be embedded.

Build the Games page. Display the following unblocked school game sites as clickable cards in a responsive grid, each with a thumbnail/icon, site name, and brief description. Clicking a card opens the site in a new proxy iframe tab: Math Playground (mathplayground.com), Cool Math Games (coolmathgames.com), Hooda Math (hoodamath.com), ABCya (abcya.com), Funbrain (funbrain.com), PBS Kids Games (pbskids.org/games), Typing Club (typingclub.com), Scratch (scratch.mit.edu), Khan Academy (khanacademy.org), Prodigy Game (prodigygame.com).

Build the AI Assistant (Homework Helper) page. Include: (1) A heading 'AI Homework Helper'; (2) A subject selector dropdown (Math, Science, English, History, Computer Science, Other); (3) A large textarea for the question; (4) A 'Get Answer' button; (5) A response area that renders the AI answer with basic markdown-like formatting (bold, code blocks, lists); (6) A loading spinner while awaiting the response; (7) A 'Copy Answer' button; (8) A scrollable history list of recent question/answer pairs. Use the Puter.js API via `window.puter.ai.chat` — include the Puter.js CDN script tag in the page.

Build the Code Editor page with three internal sub-tabs: 'Code Editor', 'Presentation Builder', and 'Website Builder'. (1) Code Editor sub-tab: embed CodeMirror (CDN), language selector (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Python), a Run/Execute button, a live preview panel for HTML/CSS/JS output, and localStorage persistence for editor content. (2) Presentation Builder sub-tab: add/delete slides each with a title, body text, and background color picker; prev/next navigation; a fullscreen 'Present' mode. (3) Website Builder sub-tab: simple component-based builder allowing users to add text blocks, image placeholders, button components, and headers; a live preview panel; note displayed: 'Public sharing only available through the Cheetah proxy'.

Build the Quick Open page. Display the following six sites as large, visually distinct cards in a grid, each with a big icon, site name, and brief description. Clicking a card opens the site in a new proxy iframe tab: TikTok (tiktok.com), YouTube (youtube.com), Wikipedia (wikipedia.org), Disney Plus (disneyplus.com), ChatGPT (chat.openai.com), Google Gemini (gemini.google.com). Use brand-representative accent colors for each card.

Implement global UX polish: smooth CSS transitions and animations on all page/tab switches and interactive elements; a loading overlay/spinner when iframes are loading; responsive layout that works across desktop and tablet widths; consistent neon glow hover states throughout; and localStorage used to persist open tabs state and settings across sessions.

Add a 'Proxy Info' section or modal in the Home/Search page that displays the built-in proxy configuration details, showing users that Cheetah 1.0 uses a CORS proxy (allorigins.win) to tunnel requests — referencing the Node.js http-proxy pattern the user submitted as context. Display a human-readable explanation such as: 'Cheetah routes all browsing through a proxy layer to bypass restrictions. Requests are forwarded via https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=<encoded-url>.' Include the target URL pattern and port (8000 conceptually) as informational text only. This is a frontend-only display change; no backend proxy server is implemented.

Improve iframe proxy loading so more websites successfully open. Use multiple CORS proxy fallbacks in priority order: first try `https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=<encoded>`, then `https://corsproxy.io/?<encoded>`, then `https://cors-anywhere.herokuapp.com/<url>`. If all proxies fail, display the error state with a direct-link fallback. Update the `useTabs` hook and `TabContent` component accordingly.

Remove all copyright notices, footer copyright text, and any '© Caffeine.ai' or similar attribution strings from every visible area of the app, including the footer in `App.tsx` and any other components that render copyright text.

Add a visible 'Panic' button in the app header (next to the existing header elements in `Header.tsx`). When activated — either by clicking the button or by pressing Cmd+P, Ctrl+P, Cmd+B, or Ctrl+B — the following behavior must occur: (1) A full-viewport white overlay is immediately rendered on top of all content, completely hiding the app; (2) A countdown timer runs for 60 seconds; (3) All user interaction with the app is blocked during this 60-second period; (4) After 60 seconds, the white overlay is automatically removed and the app returns to its normal state. The panic button itself should be styled to fit the existing amethyst/purple theme (e.g., a red or warning-colored button with neon glow). The keyboard shortcut listener must be registered globally so it works regardless of which page or element has focus.

Remove all loading spinners, loading overlays, and loading state indicators from the iframe tab browsing experience. When a URL is loaded in a tab, the iframe must appear immediately with no loading screen, no spinner, and no in-progress visual state. This applies to TabBar.tsx (remove per-tab loading indicators), TabContent.tsx (remove the loading overlay/spinner shown while the iframe loads), and useTabs.ts (remove the isLoading/loading state tracking for tabs entirely or stop using it to gate rendering).

new_feature_requests.summary: Tab-based iframe proxy browsing with address bar, navigation buttons, and Google CSE search
Unblocked games page with game site cards that open in proxy iframe tabs
AI homework helper page using Puter.js with subject selector, Q&A history, and markdown rendering
Code editor (CodeMirror/Monaco), presentation builder, and website builder as sub-tabs
Quick Open page with cards for TikTok, YouTube, Wikipedia, Disney+, ChatGPT, Gemini opening in proxy tabs
Improve proxy to make all websites open and load faster
Remove copyright notices from the app
Panic button (clickable + Cmd/Ctrl+P or B shortcut) that triggers a white screen and locks the user out for 60 seconds
Remove loading spinner/overlay for all websites in iframe tabs — load instantly with no loading state
